title: LedgerTypeValues
description: A structured representation of an organizations financial year, defining how the year is divided into accounting periods used for recording, posting, and reporting transactions.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Primary
x-enumeration-literals.description: The main accounting ledger used for official financial reporting under the required accounting framework (e.g., US GAAP). All core postings flow here.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Secondary
x-enumeration-literals.description: A parallel ledger used to maintain alternate accounting treatments (e.g., IFRS vs GAAP), or additional adjustments not stored in the primary ledger.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Statutory
x-enumeration-literals.description: Ledger required by local laws or national regulations, often different from corporate GAAP (e.g., country-specific accounting rules).

x-enumeration-literals.name: Management
x-enumeration-literals.description: Ledger used for internal management reporting, operational accounting, and performance analysis; not used for external financial reporting.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Budget
x-enumeration-literals.description: Ledger that stores budget, forecast, or planning figures; used to compare against actuals but does not hold real accounting transactions.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Tax
x-enumeration-literals.description: Ledger for tax-specific accounting entries (e.g., tax-basis depreciation, taxable income calculations) separate from GAAP/IFRS books.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Consolidation
x-enumeration-literals.description: Ledger that holds elimination entries, intercompany adjustments, and consolidated-level postings for group reporting.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Adjustment
x-enumeration-literals.description: Ledger for audit adjustments, closing adjustments, top-side entries, or correcting postings that should not appear in operational ledgers.
